commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Days, the three great festivals. His family accompanied him, though the law only lays an express injunction upon the males to attend. We find, however, that females did not neglect to come. (Calmet)
Hosts. Hebrew (Tsebaoth) of "armies "both of angels and of Israel. God is attended by myriads of angels, and decides the fate of armies. (Haydock)
This title has not appeared in the former books. The pagans took occasion from it to form their Jupiter Sabazius. (Calmet)
Elcana might go up from the height of Ramatha to Silo, which was the highest mountain round Jerusalem of all in the holy land. (Adrichom.) (Tirinus)

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
